bullets.body: **Microsoft Agent 365 generally available May 1 — $15/user/month, included in Microsoft 365 E7 SKU; built on three pillars (observe / govern / secure); tools management gives AI admins centralized view + allow/block for Microsoft MCP servers across the tenant; deeply integrated with Entra ID + Purview + Intune + Defender; June 2026 Defender adds asset-context mapping per agent (devices, MCP servers, identities, cloud resources); commercial + education tenants activate from M365 admin center immediately; GCC/GCC High late 2026, DoD early 2027** — First mainstream-priced enterprise agent control plane wired to an existing M365 admin surface. The MCP-server allow/block primitive is the missing enterprise gate against the Apr 28 ATT&CK v19 named technique "MCP server compromise".

sections.category: AI Security > CVE / KEV / Threat Intelligence (CVSS ≥ 8.0)

bullets.body: **CISA KEV May 1 — adds CVE-2026-31431 "Copy Fail" Linux kernel local privilege escalation; CVSS 7.8 but BOD-22-01 mandatory; federal remediation deadline May 15; algif_aead AF_ALG socket + splice() interaction enables a 4-byte write into kernel page cache of any readable file, corrupting in-memory representation of /usr/bin/su to yield root without modifying the on-disk binary; affects virtually every Linux distro from 2017 onward (Ubuntu 24.04 LTS, Amazon Linux 2023, RHEL 10.1, SUSE 16, Debian, Fedora, Arch); multiple in-the-wild attacks against US think tanks + European manufacturing + cloud service providers; chains with web-app/VPN-appliance RCE for initial foothold** — Largest single-vendor KEV-affected install base of 2026; 14-day federal deadline is the second-tightest of the year.

citations.label: CISA - Adds One Known Exploited Vulnerability to Catalog (May 1)

bullets.body: **CVE-2026-31431 cross-vendor disclosure — Microsoft Security Blog publishes the full chain analysis ("Copy Fail" → root via /usr/bin/su page-cache corruption); Wiz publishes universal-Linux LPE deep-dive; CERT-EU advisory 2026-005; Tenable + SUSE + AWS + Canadian Cyber Centre advisories all live as of May 1** — Cross-vendor disclosure alignment; Microsoft + Wiz are the cleanest writeups.
